name: The Wife As A Sister
notes: The same deception told three times: Abram and Pharaoh, Abraham and Abimelech, Isaac and Rebekah and Abimelech. Shared beats are linked across all three accounts where the wording or content matches; gaps are deliberate. Gen 26 has no king actually taking the wife (Abimelech sees them sporting first), and its closing charge is protection rather than the restitution found in Gen 12 and Gen 20.
tension: Whether these are three separate incidents or variant tellings of one tradition is debated, and the patriarchs' deception is a moral difficulty the text leaves unresolved.

links.label: What is this thou hast done
saidst thou, She is my sister
